pattern: Use a dynamic program where dp[i][j] is the length of the common suffix ending at nums1[i-1] and nums2[j-1], extending matches by one and tracking the maximum.
hints: A repeated subarray is contiguous in both arrays, unlike a subsequence.
Let dp[i][j] count matching elements ending exactly at those positions; it is dp[i-1][j-1]+1 when the elements match, else zero.
The answer is the largest value appearing anywhere in the dp table.

edge_inputs.description: shared run of three in the middle
common subsequence but only length-one contiguous match
repeated pattern, longest contiguous overlap matters
value bounds zero and hundred present
best match at the boundaries of the arrays

stress.description: moderate random arrays from a small alphabet to force overlaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    a = [rng.randint(0, 5) for _ in range(size)]
    b = [rng.randint(0, 5) for _ in range(size)]
    return [a, b]
